title: fix: seekToRange silently dropped when streaming poll in-flight
description:
Race condition in use-tick-poller.ts: seekToRange returns early if fetchingRef.current is true (set by streaming fetchForward poll). Since fetchForward runs every 200ms, there's a high chance seekToRange is silently dropped when user clicks Apply in the WindowRangeTrimmer.

Consequence: user enters paused mode (goToTick fires), manualWindowActive is set (streaming stops), but the range fetch never happens. User is stuck viewing stale data.

Fix approach: seekToRange must preempt in-flight streaming polls. Use a request generation counter: seekToRange increments a seekGenRef and proceeds regardless of fetchingRef. fetchForward's .then() checks if seekGenRef changed during flight and discards its result if so. seekToRange should also reset fetchingRef before starting its own fetch.

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (primary), possibly packages/react/src/hooks/useInspector.ts and BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx for edge cases.
closeReason: Fixed seekToRange race condition: added seekGenRef generation counter so seekToRange preempts in-flight streaming polls. fetchForward discards stale results in .then() and .catch() when seekGen changes.

title: fix: off-by-one in seekToRange — fromTickId excluded
description:
When user selects window starting at tick 132, the first tick returned is 133. The range query uses inclusive bounds (>=, <=) on the server, so the issue is likely in how fromTickId is computed or passed from the WindowRangeTrimmer. Check fracToTick rounding, the limit calculation (toTickId - fromTickId + 1 vs off-by-one), and the server-side findRange query.

Files: packages/react/src/components/panels/WindowRangeTrimmer.tsx (fracToTick), packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (seekToRange limit), packages/studio-server/src/infra/knex/tick-repository.ts (findRange).

title: feat: tick store size should match selected window size
description:
When user selects a window via WindowRangeTrimmer, the inspector maxTicks should equal the window size (toTickId - fromTickId + 1), not the default ringBufferSize (500). Currently the inspector always uses ringBufferSize=500, so if the user selects a window of 1000 ticks, only 500 survive ingestion. If they select 100, the store is unnecessarily large.

The fix should propagate the selected window size from onFetchTickRange through to the inspector's maxTicks. This likely means:
1. use-studio-controls.ts: expose the active window size (or null when streaming)
2. BehaviourTreeDebugger: when a manual window is active, override inspectorOptions.maxTicks with the window size
3. Alternatively, compute dynamically: when seekToRange is called with (from, to), set a windowMaxTicks = to - from + 1 and expose it alongside isLoadingWindow

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts, packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts, packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx, packages/studio-ui/src/App.tsx
closeReason: Added windowMaxTicks to StudioControls; propagated from onFetchTickRange through to App.tsx inspectorOptions.maxTicks
